search_terms_used: half marathon training physiology performance determinants systematic review pubmed
10K race performance predictors VO2max training volume pubmed
half marathon performance prediction model recreational runners pubmed
half marathon 10K pacing strategy performance level pubmed
half marathon physiological demands VO2max intensity pubmed
10K energy system contribution aerobic glycolytic pubmed
training intensity distribution RCT endurance runners pubmed
polarized training 10K recreational runners RCT pubmed
coverage_notes: Expanded coverage: original 8 papers focused on prediction models and pacing; added 6 papers covering training physiology (Williams 1983 HM at 79% VO2max), HM vs marathon demands (Del Coso 2017), 10K energy systems (Damasceno 2015), training intensity distribution RCTs (Esteve-Lanao 2007, Munoz 2014), and intensity distribution best practice (Seiler 2010). Now has 2 RCTs, 1 review, 3 cross-sectional physiology studies alongside original prediction/pacing papers. Running-specific throughout.

papers.title: Predictive Performance Models in Long-Distance Runners: A Narrative Review
papers.journal: International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health
papers.notes: Narrative review synthesizing 58 studies on predictive performance models for half-marathon and marathon, covering laboratory (VO2max, vVO2max), training, and anthropometric predictor variables.

papers.title: Predictive Variables of Half-Marathon Performance for Male Runners
papers.journal: Journal of Sports Science and Medicine
papers.notes: Established and validated predictive equations for HM performance using 78 male runners; physiological variables predicted 94.9% of variance, training variables 90.3%, anthropometric 93.7%, and combined model 96.2%.

papers.title: Predictors of half-marathon performance in male recreational athletes
papers.notes: Developed prediction formula for HM time in 134 recreational runners: Race time = 80.056 + 2.498×BMI - 0.594×VO2max - 0.191×weekly training distance (R²=0.63).

papers.title: Participation and performance characteristics in half-marathon run: a brief narrative review
papers.journal: Journal of Muscle Research and Cell Motility
papers.notes: Reviewed participation trends, performance characteristics, sex and age differences in half-marathon running, providing context for training prescription and performance expectations.

papers.title: Cooper Test Provides Better Half-Marathon Performance Prediction in Recreational Runners Than Laboratory Tests
papers.notes: Compared field (Cooper test) vs laboratory (treadmill VO2max, vVO2max) for predicting HM time in recreational runners; Cooper test distance was the best single predictor of HM performance.

papers.title: Predictor variables for a half marathon race time in recreational male runners
papers.journal: Open Access Journal of Sports Medicine
papers.notes: Identified training and anthropometric predictor variables for HM race time in 84 recreational male runners, finding speed in training and body fat most predictive.

papers.title: Effect of performance level on pacing strategy during a 10-km running race
papers.notes: Examined pacing strategy differences across performance levels in 10K races; high-performance runners adopted a fast-start strategy while low-performance runners used even pacing. Peak treadmill velocity, LT, and RE correlated with pacing choices.

papers.title: The pacing differences in performance levels of marathon and half-marathon runners
papers.notes: Analyzed pacing across 208,760 HM and marathon finishers (2006-2018); better runners showed more even pacing, HM runners paced more evenly than marathoners across all performance groups.

papers.title: Some physiological demands of a half-marathon race on recreational runners
papers.notes: Measured VO2, blood lactate, and energy expenditure during a half-marathon in recreational runners to quantify the physiological demands and determine that average race intensity was 79% VO2max.

papers.title: A comparison of the physiological demands imposed by competing in a half-marathon vs. a marathon
papers.journal: Journal of Sports Medicine and Physical Fitness
papers.notes: Compared physiological responses (muscle fatigue, fiber damage, pain, fluid/electrolyte balance) between matched pairs of half-marathoners and marathoners running at similar speeds to distinguish the demands of each distance.

papers.title: Energy system contribution in a maximal incremental test: correlations with pacing and overall performance in a 10-km running trial
papers.journal: Brazilian Journal of Medical and Biological Research
papers.notes: Assessed whether aerobic and glycolytic energy system contributions measured during an incremental treadmill test correlated with pacing and overall performance during an outdoor 10-km race.

papers.title: Impact of training intensity distribution on performance in endurance athletes
papers.journal: Journal of Strength and Conditioning Research
papers.notes: Randomly assigned 12 sub-elite runners to two 5-month programs differing in low- vs moderate-intensity volume distribution and compared effects on 10.4-km cross-country performance.

papers.title: Does polarized training improve performance in recreational runners?
papers.journal: International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance
papers.notes: Randomly assigned 30 recreational runners to polarized or between-thresholds training over 10 weeks and compared changes in 10-km race performance and physiological markers.

papers.title: What is best practice for training intensity and duration distribution in endurance athletes?
papers.journal: International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance
papers.notes: Synthesized research on training intensity distribution patterns across competitive endurance sports, establishing the ~80/20 low-to-high intensity framework as typical best practice for trained athletes.
